fall-spritz-amaro-and-dry-cider-cocktail.name: Fall Spritz (amaro And Dry Cider Cocktail)
instructions: Pour amaro and cider into a glass filled with ice and stir to combine; garnish with orange twist.

fancy-dirt-cake.name: Fancy Dirt Cake

ingredients.item: A 3.5-quart (or 8-inch) clean plastic or terra cotta flower pot or trifle bowl

instructions: Pulse cookies in a food processor until coarse crumbs form (or place in a plastic bag and pound with a rolling pin); set aside.
Pour milk into a medium saucepan; scrape in vanilla seeds and add pod. Heat over medium until just before boiling.

Whisk eggs, sugar, cornstarch, and salt in a large bowl. Whisking constantly, pour half of milk mixture into egg mixture. (Putting a wet towel underneath bowl will help stabilize it.) Add egg and milk mixture to remaining milk mixture in saucepan. Cook over medium heat, whisking constantly, until custard is thick and bubbles just a bit, about 7 minutes, then continue to cook 1 minute more.

Strain custard through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl; discard vanilla pod. Add 1/4 cup butter and whisk to combine. Cover with plastic wrap, pressing directly on surface of custard, and chill until cold, about 3 hours.
Using an electric mixer on medium-high speed, beat cream cheese and remaining 3/4 cup butter in a large bowl until smooth. Add chilled custard and beat until combined.
Using electric mixer on medium-high speed, whip cream in a medium bowl until medium peaks form. Using a rubber spatula, gently fold whipped cream into custard to combine.

Layer custard and cookie crumbs alternately in flower pot, inserting gummy worms at random, ending with crumbs. Stick a flower or two into cake to decorate, adding more worms on top of "soil" if desired.
Custard can be made 1 day ahead; keep chilled.

fasting-cake-argiro.name: Fasting Cake

ingredients.item: 300 grams of soya or coconut almond
2-3 tbsp water or coconut or almond milk
300 grams of soya or coconut almond
2-3 tbsp water or coconut or almond milk

instructions: Preheat the evening where we cook at 180°C.
Butter two round 22 cm ring uniforms or two baking pans.
We're putting paper out.
Shake all the solid iredients in a bowl, flour, baking powder and cocoa.
Add the sugar, mix, add the milk with the melted margarine and mix again.
Finally, add the trouffle, mix gently and equalize the forms.
Bake for about 35 minutes until stabilized.
Leave them to cool on a grill and then disfigure.
Cut horizontally into two pieces each.
Mix all the solid materials.
Add the Melted butter and water and mix well with wire.
Beat until it turns to whipped cream.
Heat the syrup with the cognac and let it cool.
Put a cake on a tour.
Brush some syrup and rub with cream.
Add some sour sweets.
We repeat the processes.
Finally, after putting on the last cake, brush around with the cream.
Garnish with chocolate rolls and sour sweet.
Alternatively, slide the mixture into two baking pans with a diameter of 26 cm or 28 cm.
Bake for less time, since the layerer will be thinking, and fill the two pantespanes with one layer of cream.
So more relations come out, because she won't get too tall and have a bigger surface.
The first way, of course, is more impressionive.
Freeze for 2-3 hours and serve.

fasting-moussaka-argiro.name: Fasting Moussaka

ingredients.item: 3/4 cups of potatoes for chewed potatoes
3/4 cups of potatoes for chewed potatoes

instructions: Wash and cut the eggs into slides first and place them in cold water with salt for 10 minutes, so that they do not draw too much oil into the frying.
Drain well and sip the liquids away.
Fry in olive oil until they have a light blonde color on both sides and place them on absorbent paper.
We can instead fly them, make them in the evening after oiling them lightly, so the moussakas can be lighter.
Wash and chop the mushrooms.
In a pot added olive oil, add the onions and place them on medium heat until soft for 4 minutes.
Then, add the mushrooms and let them sim mer until them drink their liquids.
Add the tomato, salt pepper and cinnamon wood.
Let them boil until the sauce dickens.

feta-cheese.name: Feta Cheese
instructions: Combine 1/4 cup water and 1/2 teaspoon calcium chloride in a small bowl; mix well.
Combine 1/4 cup water and 1/2 teaspoon rennet in another small bowl; mix well.

Pour milk into a double boiler set over medium-low heat. Heat milk until it registers 30°C (30 degrees C) on an instant-read thermometer. Sprinkle cheese culture on the surface. Allow to dissolve without stirring for a few minutes, then stir well. Cover and keep at 30°C (30 degrees C) for 1 hour.

Pour calcium chloride mixture into the milk; stir for 1 minute. Add rennet mixture; stir for 1 to 2 minutes. Let stand at room temperature until milk sets into curd, about 1 hour. Press edge of the curd gently with a palette knife or the back of a spoon to check for firmness.
Cut curd all the way to the bottom of the pot into 1-inch squares. Stir gently with a rubber spatula. Let rest, 5 to 10 minutes.
Stir curds, maintaining temperature at 30°C (30 degrees C), until they sink to the bottom of the pot, about 20 minutes. Scoop out liquid (whey) with a ladle and discard or reserve for another use.

Line 2 basket-style molds with damp cheesecloth. Ladle curds into the molds with a slotted spoon and cover with ends of cheesecloth. Place one mold on top of the other, pressing lightly. Let drain, switching the molds and stirring curds a few times, until cheese is firm, at least 4 hours.
Mix 8 cups water, sea salt, 1 teaspoon calcium chloride, and white vinegar together in a large container to make brine. Submerge cheese in brine and let soak, at least 8 hours and up to 3 weeks.

fish-kidney-with-chestnut-puree-argiro.name: Fish Kidney With Chestnut Puree

ingredients.item: 1/4 cup of balsamic vintage with honey
1 kg chestnuts cleared (we can also use ground chestnuts)
1/4 cup of balsamic vintage with honey

instructions: We clean the waters first from the fishnet.
With a baking string, tie each piece per 4 cm, depending on its size. After tying all the fish pits, cut them into a pendent to keep their shape during cooking.
Heat the olive oil in a deep pan with lid.
Salt and pepper the meat and brown it over high heat for 3-4 minutes on each side.
Add the dried fruit.
Add the wine, balsamic and cognac. Allow the liquids to Evaporate and then add the broth and thyme.
Turn the pendents with tweeters and simmer until the sauce is gleamed and somethingned. She's ready when big blisters show up.

fish-kidney-with-sweet-and-sour-glaze-ar.name: Fish Kidney With Sweet And Sour Glaze

ingredients.item: 2 tabletspoons orange juice and 1 piece of peel (to be uncorned)
2 tabletspoons orange juice and 1 piece of peel (to be uncorned)

instructions: Leave the meat for 30 minutes on the kitchen counter.
With a sharp knife, clear the fishnet from fibers, membranes and fat.
Place in bowl the ingredients of the Glaze: honey, sugar, juice, orange peel, brandy, cinnamon, clove, nutmeg and cayenne pepper.
Add the ingredients to the baking bowl and mix with a sponge.
Spread the solid materials on a baking pan or tray.
Tampon the meat very well with abstract paper to dry.
Then roll a fishnet into the solid materials until it is complete covered (i.e., fry as flour a meat for frying) and shake off the unnecessary baking.
In a non-stick pan, heat 2 tabletspoons of olive oil and in medium to high heat, add the pants for 7 to 10 minutes, until they make a nice crust and have a nice color around.
We make sure we don't stick them together.
After they're pink, we put them on a platter.
Remove any fat from the pan and add the activeients of the glaze over medium heat.
Rub the bottom so the Caramelized juices of the beat can deflabour.
Simmer for 2 minutes until halved.
We hold 2 tabletspoons of glaze for later and put the meat on a grill in a baking pan.
With a brush, garnish around each fish nefrie, all of it.
Bake in a preheated oven at 180°C, in residence, for about 12 to 15 minutes.
Add the two fish-freaks and continue baking for 5 minutes.
Once they are removed from the oven, brush once more with the glaze and allow them to stand for 10 minutes.
Add the honey and mustard and mix well.
Add 1 tablespoon to the mustard glaze.
Add the meat into a cutting wood and cut it into something slides (medaillon).
Serve with the rest of the Glaze on the table.

instructions: We put a tablespoon of extra virgin olive oil into a frying pan and scattering the peas. Let's have a reservation.

We shredded the onion and put it in a frying pan with extra virgin olive oil and a little salt, fry it for 8 minutes. After time we add the two laminated garlic, we mix well and leave 10 to 15 minutes till the onion caramelates (at medium low heat).
Once the onion is at its point, we add the chorizo cut into pieces and we turn around. We put the sweet peppermint and mix well for a minute. Below we add vinegar and let cook for another minute.
We scrubbed the tomato and put them into the frying pan, let them cook for 15 minutes. After 15 minutes, we added ham and peas. We let them cook with low heat while we fry our potatoes and eggs.

Peel and cut into small pieces the potatoes, put them into a saucer and add sunflower oil to fry (you can also do with olive oil). We fry at medium low heat, till they are tender inside and golden outside. Once you have been fried, we get them out of the saucer and leave our potatoes to rest on kitchen absorbing paper to remove excess fat.
Finally, we fry three eggs (one per dinner) into extra virgin olive oil and add some salt, we reserve.
To put these Andalusian eggs to flamenca, we distribute potatoes among the three plates, above we put tomato sauce with chorizo, peas and ham and finally we add the fried egg.

instructions: Remove the stems and seeds from the peppers first.

The pepper dip is ready to be stored in a glass jar and kept in your refrigerator.

french-cookies-belgi-galettes.name: French Cookies (belgi Galettes)
instructions: Stir brown sugar and butter together in a large bowl until smooth and creamy. Add eggs and vanilla; beat well. Gradually stir in flour. Set bowl over a hot water bath; stir to combine. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Preheat the galette iron according to manufacturer's instructions.

Roll dough into 1-inch balls; place in galette iron. Close the iron; cook cookies, about 30 seconds in an electric galette iron, or 2 to 4 minutes in a handheld iron over medium heat. Carefully remove cookies and cool on wire racks.

french-toast-in-air-fryer-pequerecetas.name: French Toast In Air Fryer

ingredients.item: Mold bread, calves bread or a bar of bread from the previous day.
One piece of lemon and another piece of orange shell
Una ramita de canela en rama
Papel de hornear para freidora de aire

instructions: We cut the bread into thick cuts, about 2cm. We have used a homemade mold bread that we had been with but you can use homemade or bought calves bread, brioche bread or a bread bar from the previous day. It's better to use bread from the previous day or even two days earlier as it better absorbs the infusion milk and will not get rid of it so easily.

Disponemos el pan en una fuente amplia y con algo de profundidad donde nos quepan unas cuantas rebanadas. Dependiendo de la cantidad de torrijas que vayamos a hacer y de lo grande que sea la fuente, tendremos que trabajar por turnos a la hora de empapar el pan en la leche.

Then we'll get our milk mixed up. We washed the lemon and the orange well and we peeled them, trying to remove the white part as it greatly bites. In a bowl or pot we warm milk with 100 grams of sugar, an orange and lemon skin and a cinnamon rambling with a wooden spoon so that the sugar will be diluted. That's the most traditional infusion, but you can vary it at your best, omitting some of the ingredients or replacing cinnamon with a vanilla pod. Before you get to a boil, you will remove and leave at rest for 10 minutes so that the flavors will be well mixed and cold a little.

It's important to let the milk cool, as if it's very hot the calves will be broken very quickly. Let's get our milk flavoured up above our calves and let them get well soaked from their milk before we turn them around with both hands with care that they don't get broken. The time that the calves have to be soaked in milk will depend on how much bread we have been using, how high they are cuts or how high their milk temperature can be from about 10 seconds to about 10 minutes for them to get their milk and its flavors well absorbed. The flavoured milk that we have had and we reserve for later use.

At this point, we preheat the air fryer for 5 minutes at 180 °. Meanwhile, we beat our eggs into a deep bowl. Be sure they don't get rid of them, we remove the slice of bread from the milk and put them through the scrambling eggs.

Once preheated, we place baking paper for air frying into the bucket and spray with some oil. That'll help to turn the calves around. With care to avoid breaking, with both hands we put our calves into the air fryer. We'll just put up with them and leave some space between them. In our case and as they were big cuts, we have made them two at a time. We program the air fryer at 180 ° for 12 minutes, after this time we turn them and program another 10 minutes at 180 °, and check that they are golden at our best.

We mix the sugar with the cinnamon powder into a large plate and bounce the calves still hot into the mixture. If we want to have them, we can have them that way, but air-frying calves are somewhat drier than oil-fried calves and so for calves to get juicy, a trick is to have them bathed slightly with a few tablespoons of the infusion milk we had booked. That's how we get juicy. We can even pack them for a few minutes in milk before bouncing them with cinnamon sugar.

fresh-chile-hot-sauce.name: Fresh-chile Hot Sauce
instructions: Pulse chiles, garlic, and salt in a food processor to a fine paste. Transfer to a glass jar; cover with cheesecloth and secure with a rubber band. Let sit at room temperature 5–7 days to ferment (the longer it sits, the more pronounced the flavor will be).

Transfer chile mixture to a blender; add vinegar, lime juice, and sugar and purée until smooth. Transfer to a clean jar, cover with cheesecloth, and secure with rubber band. Let sit at room temperature at least 2 days and up to 5 days to ferment more.
Transfer hot sauce to blender and blend again, then strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean jar. Cover and chill.
Do Ahead: Hot sauce can be made 6 months ahead. Keep chilled.

fried-anchovy-argiro.name: Fried Anchovy
instructions: Clean the anchovy by pulling the head and leaving the instinctines.
Wash him well and put him in a colander to draw well.
Salt it all around and mix, shaking the colander to go everywhere.
Add the flower to a bowl and add it in doses.
Shake well floured. Add to a colander and shake off the excess flour.
Alternatively flour it in a bag of food gradually and shake off the excess flour before frying.
In a deep pan, add the oil to cover about 2 cm in height. Let it burn well.
Add a few pieces and lower the volume.
We don't mix often. Allow them to brown around for about 2-3 minutes.
Mix 1-2 times, if you want it to become crispy.
All that is browned, we pour a dribbled sponge into a tray with abstract paper to draw the excess oil.

fried-peach-hand-pies.name: Fried Peach Hand Pies

ingredients.prep: peeled, cut into  0.25" pieces, or 1 lb. frozen, slightly thawed peaches cut into  0.25" pieces

ingredients.item: Diamond crystal or  0.25 tsp. morton kosher salt
. diamond crystal or  0.5 tsp. morton kosher salt

instructions:
Cook 1 lb. peaches (about 4), peeled, cut into ¼" pieces, or 1 lb. frozen, slightly thawed peaches cut into ¼" pieces, ⅓ cup granulated sugar, 1 tsp. finely grated lemon zest, 1 tsp. fresh lemon juice, and ½ tsp. Diamond Crystal or ¼ tsp. Morton kosher salt in a medium heavy saucepan over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally with a heatproof rubber spatula, until peaches are softened, juices are thickened, and mixture resembles a chunky jam, 45–60 minutes. Let cool.
Do ahead: Filling can be made 1 week ahead. Cover and chill.

While the peaches are cooking, whisk 2 tsp. granulated sugar, 1 tsp. Diamond Crystal or ½ tsp. Morton kosher salt, and 2½ cups (313 g) all-purpose flour in a large bowl until combined. Using a pastry cutter or your fingers, work in ½ cup (1 stick) chilled unsalted butter, cut into 1" pieces, until largest pieces are pea-size. (Alternatively, pulse together dry ingredients in a food processor, then pulse in butter.) Sprinkle 2 Tbsp. apple cider vinegar over and mix with your hands to combine. Drizzle ¼ cup plus 3 Tbsp. ice water over and mix by hand until dough just comes together in a shaggy mass.
Turn out dough onto a lightly floured surface and gather into a ball. Divide in half and pat into disks; wrap each in plastic. Chill at least 15 minutes.

Working one disk at a time, unwrap dough and roll out on a lightly floured surface to ⅛" thick. Using a 5"-diameter cutter or bowl, punch out as many rounds as you can, transferring to a baking sheet as you go. Gather dough scraps, reroll, and punch out more rounds. (You should get 12 or 13 in all.)

Working with 1 round at a time, spoon a heaping tablespoonful of filling onto 1 side. Brush edges with water, then fold empty side of dough up and over filling and bring edges together to create a half-moon shape. Crimp edges firmly with a fork to seal. Arrange pies on a parchment-lined baking sheet and chill until firm, about 15 minutes.
Fit a large heavy skillet, preferably cast iron, with thermometer and pour in vegetable oil to come ½" up sides. Heat over medium-high until thermometer registers 340°.

Working in 3 or 4 batches, keeping remaining pies chilled until ready to fry, and adding more oil between batches if needed, fry pies, turning every 2 minutes with tongs and adjusting heat as needed to maintain oil temperature, until golden brown and crisp, 13–15 minutes; if you see any pies beginning to burst open toward the end of cooking, immediately remove from oil. Transfer to paper towels to drain.

Just before serving, transfer pies to a platter and dust with powdered sugar.
Do ahead: Dough can be made 1 day ahead. Keep chilled.

fried-potatoes-with-eggplants-argiro.name: Fried Potatoes With Eggplants
instructions: Wash and remove the step from the eggplants.
Cut them into large bites and leave them in a bowl of water for 10 minutes.
Clean the potatoes and cut them to four. If you want, you can add pumpkins.
Drain the eggs from the water.
Heat the olive oil and sauté the garlic in a pot for 2 minutes.
Add the potatoes and eggs and mix the ingredients to oil.
Add the tomato paste and sugar.
Fill with water just covering the food, about 11⁄2 cup.
Add salt and pepper to the raspberry and add the bay leaf.
Simmer the yoghurt for 25 minutes, until the potatoes soften well and the sauce thinks.
When the food is Ripe, it is ready to enjoy it.
Always serve it warm with feta cheese or fresh cheese.

fries-suikerbrood-frisian-sugar-bread.name: Fries Suikerbrood (frisian Sugar Bread)
instructions: Combine milk, 1 tablespoon sugar, and yeast in a small bowl. Let stand until yeast is frothy, about 10 minutes.
Whisk egg in a small bowl. Place 1 tablespoon in a separate bowl; reserve for glazing.
Sift flour into a large bowl; mix in salt. Add yeast mixture, beaten egg, 2 tablespoons ginger syrup, and 1 1/2 tablespoon melted butter. Knead until a smooth dough forms.
Grease a large bowl with 1 teaspoon oil. Place dough in the greased bowl. Cover with a clean dish towel and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 hour.
Grease a 2-pound disposable loaf pan with remaining 1 teaspoon oil. Sprinkle remaining 1 tablespoon sugar and ginger syrup over the bottom and sides of the pan.
Mix pearl sugar, 1 tablespoon melted butter, and cinnamon together in a bowl.

Roll dough into a rectangle on a lightly floured work surface. Sprinkle 1/2 of the pearl sugar mixture on top; knead until evenly incorporated. Roll out again and add remaining pearl sugar mixture; knead until evenly incorporated.
Shape dough into a loaf; place seam-side down in the prepared pan. Cover with a damp cloth and let rise until puffy, about 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 191°C (190 degrees C). Brush top of loaf with reserved beaten egg.
Bake in the preheated oven until top is golden brown, about 30 minutes. Cool slightly in the pan before slicing, about 10 minutes.

frog-cupcakes.name: Frog Cupcakes
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Line two 12-cup muffin tins with paper liners.

Mix cake mix water, oil, and egg whites in a large bowl with an electric mixer on low speed until moistened, then mix on medium speed for 2 minutes. Pour batter into the prepared muffin cups, filling each 1/2 full.
Bake in the preheated oven until tops spring back when lightly pressed, 19 to 23 minutes. Cool in the tins for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack and let cool completely, about 20 minutes more.

While the cupcakes are cooling, stir 2/3 of the frosting with green food coloring in a small bowl until well combined. Place remaining frosting in another small bowl and set aside. Cut each marshmallow in fourths widthwise to make four thin circles. Place green sugar in a shallow bowl. Set out a very small bowl of water.
Frost cooled cupcakes with green frosting and sprinkle with some green sugar.

Wet half of each marshmallow circle with water, then dip the wet part into green sugar to coat. Place two dipped marshmallows onto each cupcake for the frog's eyes; the green sugar should be facing up for the eyelids. Use a tiny bit of white frosting to glue a chocolate chip onto the center of each marshmallow for the pupils.
Stir red food coloring into remaining white icing. Transfer to a plastic bag. Snip off one corner of the bag and pipe pink icing onto cupcakes to draw mouths and nostrils as desired.

frojitos.name: Frojitos
instructions: In a kettle or microwave, heat the water until nearly boiling. Add the sugar and stir until it dissolves completely. Raw sugar needs more time to dissolve than granulated sugar. If it’s taking a while, reheat it and resume stirring. Let cool to room temperature before using. Makes approximately 7 ounces.

In a blender, combine rum, lime juice, Raw Sugar Syrup, and mint leaves and add 2 cups of ice. Blend until smooth and divide between two glasses of choice. Lightly tap the mint sprigs against your hand to release their aromatic oils before adding to the glasses.

fruit-crisp.name: Fruit Crisp

ingredients.prep: raspberries, peaches, plums, or cherries), trimmed and chopped into bite-size pieces

instructions: 1. Preheat the oven to 177°C. Place six ramekins on a baking sheet and set aside.
2. In a large bowl, gently toss together the fruit, sugar, and lemon zest and let rest for 5 minutes.

3. In a medium bowl, combine the flour, oats, salt, brown sugar, walnuts, and butter. Using your hands or a spoon, gently toss to blend the ingredients and then rub the mixture together to work the butter into walnut-size lumps.
4. Divide the fruit between the ramekins, gently packing it down. Divide the oat and walnut streusel topping between the ramekins, packing a hearty mound on top of each.
5. Bake the fruit crisps until the streusel is golden brown, 18 to 20 minutes. Let rest 10 minutes before serving.

7. Adding plenty of garlic to the cheeks, you can add the flavor powered.
8. You can also get a fragrant and spacious pickled by placing slices of lemon.

pumpkin-chulas-or-calacu-traditional-rec.name: Fumpkin Chula Or Calacú (traditional Recipes From Galicia)
pumpkin-chulas-or-calacu-traditional-rec.original_name: Chulas de calabaza o calacú (receta tradicional de Galicia)

ingredients.item: 1 / 2 about (8 g) of chemical yeast (royal type)
A pint of ground cinnamon (you can buy them here)

instructions: To start with we have to cook pumpkin. We have cooked it in the microwave: cut into pieces we put it into a bowl with a cover suitable for microwave as this and cook 10 minutes at maximum power. The time will depend on the size of the pieces, if necessary cook some more minutes. If you do not have a bowl with a microwave cover, you can use a plate and cover with a microwave-suitable kitchen film, or wrap well with baked paper. We can also cut them into slices and cook them into an oven at 180 ″ 3 ″ for about 20 minutes or till they are tender, they have to be dyed. Once we have a tender pumpkin, we run out of water that I have been able to release and we make purée.
We let pumpkin purée get some cold. We added the eggs and the sugar and we bawked to mix everything up very well.

In another bowl we sig the flour with the yeast, salt and cinnamon ground. Let's put the flour mixture into the pumpkin purée bowl with an egg and beat with a manual beaker till the dough's quite homogenous.

In an anti-stick frying pan we heat up an oil chorrit, we can distribute with a kitchen brush. When the frying pan's hot we'll put a tablespoon of dough back and forth with care to give a round shape to the pancake. Depending on the size of the frying pan we can make two or three pits at once. We let them cook about two minutes with medium heat, as we see that the surface of the pits begins to make bubbles, with a scatula we turn and let them cook another minute.

When they're cooked, we'll get the pits from the frying pan to a plate. We keep cooking the rest of the dough till we finish and put some oil back into the frying pan if necessary. At the end we can sprinkle them with some sugar, we have used both regular granulated sugar and glas and they are quite good both ways.
